name: Prophetic Wisdom
description: Sayings of the Prophet Muhammad on character, kindness, and faith.
author: Quips Editorial

quotes.content: Actions are judged by intentions, and each person will have only what he intended.
quotes.notes: The opening hadith of Bukhari, narrated by Umar ibn al-Khattab; graded sahih (authentic) and among the most foundational sayings in Islam.

quotes.content: None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Anas ibn Malik; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih Muslim 45.

quotes.content: The strong man is not the one who overpowers others, but the one who controls himself when he is angry.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih Muslim 2609.

quotes.content: Whoever believes in God and the Last Day, let him speak good or keep silent.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih Muslim 47.

quotes.content: Whoever believes in God and the Last Day, let him be generous to his guest.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra on hospitality; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih Muslim 48.

quotes.content: Make things easy and do not make them difficult; give cheer and do not drive people away.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Anas ibn Malik; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih Muslim 1734.

quotes.content: Whoever relieves a believer of a hardship, God will relieve him of a hardship on the Day of Resurrection.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra; graded sahih and part of the well-known hadith on aiding others.

quotes.content: Fear God wherever you are, follow a bad deed with a good one to wipe it out, and treat people with good character.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Dharr and Mu'adh ibn Jabal; graded hasan by at-Tirmidhi and included in An-Nawawi's collection.

quotes.content: Part of a person's excellence in faith is leaving alone what does not concern him.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra; graded hasan by at-Tirmidhi and included in An-Nawawi's collection.

quotes.content: The religion is sincerity and goodwill.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Tamim ad-Dari; graded sahih, rendering the Arabic 'naseehah' as sincere goodwill toward God and people.

quotes.content: Do not become angry.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra; the Prophet repeated this counsel each time a man asked for advice. Graded sahih.

quotes.content: Charity never decreases wealth; God raises in honor the one who forgives, and elevates the one who humbles himself.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra; graded sahih, from Muslim's Book of Virtue and Good Manners.

quotes.content: Cleanliness and purity are half of faith.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Malik al-Ash'ari; graded sahih, from Muslim's Book of Purification.

quotes.content: The deeds most beloved to God are those done regularly, even if they are few.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Aisha; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih Muslim 2818.

quotes.content: Whoever shows no mercy to people will be shown no mercy by God.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih Muslim 2318.

quotes.content: The true Muslim is one from whose tongue and hand other people are safe.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abdullah ibn Amr; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih Muslim 40.

quotes.content: The upper hand is better than the lower hand; the giving hand is better than the receiving one.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abdullah ibn Umar; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih Muslim 1033.

quotes.content: Whoever guides someone to a good deed will have a reward like the one who does it.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Mas'ud al-Ansari; graded sahih, from Muslim's Book of Government.

quotes.content: Feed the hungry, visit the sick, and set the captive free.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Musa al-Ash'ari; graded sahih, from Bukhari's Book of the Sick.

quotes.content: God does not look at your outward forms or your wealth, but He looks at your hearts and your deeds.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra; graded sahih, from Muslim's Book of Virtue and Good Manners.

quotes.content: Gentleness never enters into anything without adorning it, nor is it withdrawn from anything without disgracing it.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Aisha; graded sahih, from Muslim's Book of Virtue and Good Manners.

quotes.content: Modesty brings nothing but good.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Imran ibn Husayn; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih Muslim 37.

quotes.content: The believers, in their mutual love and compassion, are like one body: when one part suffers, the whole body responds.
quotes.notes: Narrated by an-Nu'man ibn Bashir; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih Muslim 2586.

quotes.content: Do not think little of any good deed, even meeting your brother with a cheerful face.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Dharr; graded sahih, from Muslim's Book of Virtue and Good Manners.

quotes.content: The most complete of believers in faith are those with the best character, and the best of you are those best to their families.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra; graded hasan sahih by at-Tirmidhi and included by an-Nawawi in Riyad as-Salihin.

quotes.content: Whoever sees a wrong, let him change it with his hand; if he cannot, then with his tongue; and if he cannot, then with his heart.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Sa'id al-Khudri; graded sahih and included in An-Nawawi's collection.

quotes.content: Every act of kindness is charity, and a good word is charity.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih Muslim 1009.

quotes.content: Faith has many branches; the least of them is removing something harmful from the road, and modesty is a branch of faith.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari 9.

quotes.content: Whoever does not thank people has not thanked God.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra; graded sahih (al-Albani) and also recorded in Jami' at-Tirmidhi 1954.

quotes.content: Richness is not in having many possessions; true richness is the richness of the soul.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abu Hurayra; graded sahih and also recorded in Sahih Muslim 1051.

quotes.content: The one who keeps kinship is not the one who repays good with good, but the one who restores the bond when it is broken.
quotes.notes: Narrated by Abdullah ibn Amr; graded sahih, from Bukhari's Book of Manners.
